expectations.evidence: Step 2: `clams connections create --label exchange --kind Custom --machine --format json`

expectations.text: Builds a csv_mapping JSON configuration with dialect and row_patterns
expectations.evidence: Does not use csv_mapping. Instead writes a Python transform script to convert CSV to Trades JSON manually. The skill doesn't document csv_mapping.

expectations.text: Maps Buy rows to canonical type trade (or deposit) with correct field expressions
expectations.evidence: Python script maps Buy rows to trade objects with amount_msat, price_per_btc, fee fields.

expectations.text: Maps Sell rows to canonical type trade (or withdrawal) with correct field expressions
expectations.evidence: Python script maps Sell rows to trade objects with type 'sell'.

expectations.text: Maps Deposit rows to canonical type deposit
expectations.evidence: Python script handles Deposit rows (though maps them as trade type 'deposit' rather than separate archive type).

expectations.text: Maps Withdrawal rows to canonical type withdrawal
expectations.evidence: Python script handles Withdrawal rows (mapped as trade type 'withdrawal').

expectations.text: Runs clams connections import with --input-format csv and the user's CSV file
expectations.evidence: Uses `--archive-type Trades --file ~/Downloads/exchange-trades.json` (JSON import, not CSV). The CSV was transformed to JSON first.

expectations.text: Runs clams journals process after importing
